# Lando Framework

A full-stack web framework for Gleam + Lustre with file-based routing. Write page modules, run the codegen, get a working web app. Opinionated: Lustre for UI, SQLite for data, Marmot for queries, ETF for wire format.

## Quick start

```sh
bash bin/new my_app
cd my_app && bin/dev
```

`bin/dev` runs the lando codegen (scans `src/pages/`, generates router, dispatch, SSR handler, WS handler, and client package), builds the JS client, and starts the Mist server on port 8080.

## Page module contract

A page file in `src/pages/` maps directly to a URL route. The filename determines the route:

| File | URL | Route variant |
|---|---|---|
| `home_.gleam` | `/` | `Home` |
| `about.gleam` | `/about` | `About` |
| `products/id_.gleam` | `/products/:id` | `ProductsId(id: Int)` |

Trailing underscore marks a dynamic segment. Names ending in `_id` become `Int` params; everything else `String`.

### What a page exports

```gleam
// src/pages/home_.gleam

// -- Types --
pub type Model { Model }
pub type Msg { SmashedLikeButton; GotServerMsg(ToClient) }
pub type ToServer { SmashLike }
pub type ToClient { NewSmashedLikes(count: Int) }
pub type ServerModel { ServerModel }

// -- Client functions (with ClientContext) --
pub fn init(client_context: ClientContext) -> #(Model, Effect(Msg))
// Pages with route params receive them after client_context:
// pub fn init(client_context: ClientContext, slug: String) -> #(Model, Effect(Msg))
pub fn update(client_context: ClientContext, model: Model, msg: Msg) -> #(Model, Effect(Msg))
pub fn view(client_context: ClientContext, model: Model) -> Element(Msg)

// -- Server functions --
pub fn server_update(model: ServerModel, msg: ToServer, server_context: ServerContext) -> #(ServerModel, Effect(ToClient))
pub fn server_init(server_context: ServerContext) -> #(ServerModel, Effect(ToClient))
pub fn load(server_context: ServerContext) -> Result(Model, LoadError)  // optional, for SSR
```

- `Model`/`Msg` — client-side state and UI events
- `init`/`update`/`view` — standard Lustre TEA (The Elm Architecture); receive `ClientContext` as first arg when a `client_context.gleam` module exists. For pages with dynamic route segments (e.g. `slug_.gleam`), `init` also receives the parsed route param after `client_context`
- `ToServer` — messages the client sends to the server over WebSocket
- `ToClient` — messages the server sends back to the client
- `GotServerMsg(ToClient)` — the single Msg variant that handles ALL server responses. In the generated client views.gleam, the constructor is prefixed per page (e.g. `HomeGotServerMsg(HomeToClient)`) to avoid name collisions
- `server_update` — runs on the BEAM, has access to DB/session via `ServerContext`
- `server_init` — returns the initial `ServerModel` + optional initial effect (e.g. sending current state to client)
- `ServerModel` — per-session server state
- `load` — SSR data loading, runs server-side before first render
- `ServerContext` — defined in `server_context.gleam`, holds server dependencies (DB connection, etc.)
- `ClientContext` — defined in `client_context.gleam`, holds cross-page client state (updated via `send_to_client_context`)
- `layout` — if `src/pages/layout.gleam` exists and exports `pub fn layout(client_context: ClientContext, content: Element(msg)) -> Element(msg)`, all pages are wrapped in it. The layout receives the current `ClientContext` so it can show auth-dependent UI (nav links, etc.)

### Effect primitives

Page modules import from `lando_runtime/effect`:

- `send_to_server(variant)` — sends a `ToServer` message to the server over WebSocket. On the client, the generated views.gleam contains per-page wrappers (e.g. `home_send_to_server`) that call `transport.send_to_server("Home", msg)` via `rpc_ffi.mjs`. The page's adapted `update` source has `lando_effect.send_to_server(` automatically rewritten to the page-specific wrapper. On the server this is a no-op.
- `send_to_client(variant)` — encodes the `ToClient` message as an ETF push frame and queues it for the WebSocket handler to send. Uses process dictionary for handler state. Useful for sending initial state on connect (e.g. from `server_init`).
- `broadcast_to_page(variant)` — broadcasts a `ToClient` push frame to ALL connections viewing the same page (via pg topic). Used for real-time updates that all viewers should see.
- `send_to_client_context(variant)` — sends a `ClientContextMsg` from a page to update the shared `ClientContext`. Allows server responses to propagate to cross-page state.
- `navigate(path)` — pushes a new browser history entry and triggers a route change via modem's popstate listener. On the server this is a no-op.
- `get_ws_session()` — returns the session ID for the current WebSocket connection (server-side only, from process dictionary). Used by pages to look up the authenticated user.
- `effect.none()` — no-op effect
- `effect.from(fn)` — create a custom effect

## Project structure

```
my_app/
├── src/
│   ├── app.gleam           # server entry point (generated once, customizable)
│   ├── server_context.gleam # ServerContext type (DB, secrets)
│   ├── client_context.gleam # ClientContext type (cross-page client state)
│   ├── pages/              # route = filesystem
│   ├── sql/                # marmot .sql files
│   └── generated/          # lando codegen output (never edit)
├── client/                 # JS client (fully generated)
├── gleam.toml
└── bin/dev
```

## What lando generates

Running `gleam run -m lando` produces:

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/generated/router.gleam` | `Route` type, `parse_route`, `route_to_path`, `href` |
| `src/generated/page_dispatch.gleam` | `PageModel`/`PageMsg` unions, init/update/view dispatch |
| `src/generated/server_dispatch.gleam` | `handle_message` dispatcher + `init_server_model` per-page init |
| `src/generated/ssr_handler.gleam` | SSR entry: takes parsed `Route`, calls `load`, renders `view` wrapped in layout, embeds model as flags |
| `src/generated/ws_handler.gleam` | WebSocket upgrade handler: ETF frame loop, session model storage, push frame delivery, topic auto-join (app/session/page), broadcast frame forwarding |
| `client/src/generated/app.gleam` | Lustre SPA entry point: per-page TEA loop (PageModel/PageMsg unions, init_page/update_page/render_page dispatch), WebSocket transport, modem-based route-change handling, push handler dispatch, optional layout wrapping (when any route has a layout_module) |
| `client/src/generated/transport.gleam` | FFI bridge to `rpc_ffi.mjs`: WebSocket send, push handler registration, ETF codec |
| `client/src/generated/views.gleam` | Per-page Model/Msg types, per-page `send_to_server` transport wrappers, and adapted init/update/view functions with prefixed types |
| `client/src/generated/types.gleam` | Mirrored page ToServer/ToClient types for the client |
| `client/src/generated/codec.gleam` | Typed encode/decode wrappers for page message types, push decoder FFI bindings |
| `client/src/generated/codec_ffi.mjs` | JS typed decoders for ETF-serialized custom types, push decoders per page |
| `client/src/generated/rpc_ffi.mjs` | ETF codec + WebSocket client (copied from lando_runtime) |

## Architecture

### Codegen tool (`src/lando/`)

| Module | Role |
|---|---|
| `lando.gleam` | CLI entry point, reads `[tools.lando]` from gleam.toml, runs pipeline |
| `scanner.gleam` | Recursively walks pages directory, produces `List(ScannedRoute)` |
| `parser.gleam` | Glance AST parser: extracts ToServer/ToClient types with full field info, detects function signatures |
| `walker.gleam` | BFS type graph walker: discovers all reachable custom types from page message types (from libero) |
| `generator.gleam` | Route type + parse_route + route_to_path + page dispatch codegen |
| `generator/client.gleam` | Client package generation: gleam.toml, transport.gleam, app.gleam (with per-page TEA loop: PageModel/PageMsg types, init_page/update_page/render_page dispatchers), rpc_ffi.mjs, decoders_prelude.mjs |
| `generator/codec.gleam` | ETF codec generation: types.gleam (mirrored page types), codec.gleam (typed wrappers), codec_ffi.mjs (JS decoders), views.gleam (adapts real page init/update/view functions with prefixed types, generates per-page `send_to_server` transport wrappers) |
| `generator/server_dispatch.gleam` | Server dispatch: `handle_message` + `init_server_model` |
| `generator/ssr_handler.gleam` | SSR handler |
| `generator/ws_handler.gleam` | WebSocket frame loop: auto-joins pg topics (app, session, page), decodes call envelopes, dispatches to server_update, sends push frames, forwards broadcast frames via Mist selector |
| `format.gleam` | `gleam format` runner for generated code |
| `field_type.gleam` | Type representation (from libero) |
| `gen_error.gleam` | Terminal error formatting (from libero) |

### Runtime library (`src/lando_runtime/`)

| Module | Role |
|---|---|
| `effect.gleam` | `send_to_server`, `send_to_client`, `broadcast_to_page`/`broadcast_to_app`/`broadcast_to_session` (ETF push via process dict + pg), `send_to_client_context` (page-to-context messaging), `navigate` (client-side URL push via modem) |
| `session.gleam` | Session cookie generation and extraction (`generate_id`, `extract_session_id`, `set_cookie_header`) |
| `topics.gleam` | Topic pub/sub using OTP `pg` process groups: join/leave/broadcast/receive for cross-process push delivery |
| `wire.gleam` | ETF encode/decode, call envelope parsing, frame tagging, variant tagging, Dynamic coercion |
| `codec.gleam` | Base64 ETF serialization for SSR flags |
| `ssr.gleam` | HTML rendering via `element.to_document_string` |
| `remote_data.gleam` | Typed async state (`NotAsked`/`Loading`/`Failure`/`Success`) |
| `error.gleam` | RPC error types |
| `trace.gleam` | Panic catching + trace IDs (from libero) |
| `rpc_ffi.mjs` | JS ETF codec + WebSocket client with auto-reconnect (1180 lines, from libero) |
| `lando_effect_ffi.mjs` | JS FFI for client-side effects: `navigate` (pushState + popstate dispatch) |
| `lando_runtime_ffi.erl` | Erlang FFI: encode/decode, process dict state for WS handler, push frame accumulator |
| `lando_runtime_wire_ffi.erl` | Erlang FFI: `decode_call` envelope parser, `variant_tag` extraction |
| `lando_runtime_topics_ffi.erl` | Erlang FFI: `pg`-based topic pub/sub (start, join, leave, members, broadcast, receive_frame) |

### Broadcast and Topics

Four-level server-to-client messaging using OTP `pg` process groups:

| Effect | Scope | pg topic |
|---|---|---|
| `broadcast_to_app(msg)` | Every connection | `"app"` |
| `broadcast_to_page(msg)` | Every connection on the current page | `"page:<page_name>"` |
| `broadcast_to_session(msg)` | Every connection in the same browser session | `"session:<session_id>"` |
| `send_to_client(msg)` | One specific connection | Direct (process dict queue) |

On WebSocket connect, the framework auto-subscribes to three implicit topics: `app`, `page:<name>`, `session:<id>`. Session is cookie-based (not auth/user-based). Custom topics can be added via `topics.join`/`topics.leave`/`topics.broadcast`.

### Transport

- **Wire format:** ETF (Erlang External Term Format), binary
- **Call envelope:** `{module_name, request_id, msg}` for client→server messages
- **Response frame:** `<<0x00, request_id:32, response_etf>>` — sent after each dispatch so the client's request/response matching doesn't timeout
- **Push frame:** `<<0x01, {module_name, msg}_etf>>` — server→client push messages (from `send_to_client`/`broadcast`)
- **Transport:** WebSocket with auto-reconnect (via `rpc_ffi.mjs`, from libero)
- **SSR:** First request renders HTML with serialized model embedded as flags
- **Hydration:** Client reads flags, boots Lustre, takes over as SPA
- **Server effects:** `send_to_client` encodes and queues push frames in the process dictionary. The WS handler drains and sends them after each dispatch via `mist.send_binary_frame`. `broadcast` sends push frames to all connections in a topic via pg groups; the WS handler's Mist selector catches these messages and forwards them as `Custom` frames.

## Dependencies

- `lustre` — UI framework (TEA, SSR, VDOM)
- `mist` — HTTP/WebSocket server (BEAM)
- `marmot` — SQL-to-Gleam codegen (introspects SQLite)
- `sqlight` — SQLite NIF
- `glance` — Gleam AST parser
- `tom` — TOML config parser
- `simplifile` — Filesystem operations
- `modem` — client-side URL routing for Lustre (generated client dependency)

## Configuration

In `gleam.toml`:

```toml
[tools.lando]
pages_root = "src/pages"
output_route = "src/generated/router.gleam"
output_dispatch = "src/generated/page_dispatch.gleam"
output_server_dispatch = "src/generated/server_dispatch.gleam"
output_ssr = "src/generated/ssr_handler.gleam"
output_ws = "src/generated/ws_handler.gleam"
sql_dir = "src/sql"
client_root = "client"
```

The lando package path is resolved from `[dependencies].lando.path` in the consumer's gleam.toml, defaulting to `".."`.

## Examples

- `examples/likes/` — real-time broadcast with ClientContext and ServerContext, SQLite persistence, demonstrates `broadcast_to_page`, `send_to_client`, and `send_to_client_context`
- `examples/realworld/` — RealWorld (Conduit) clone: full CRUD app with auth, articles, comments, tags, favorites, follows. Tests cover password hashing, slug generation, datetime formatting, and full schema validation with birdie snapshots

## Prior art

- elm-land: file-based routing conventions
- Lamdera: explicit `ToServer`/`ToClient` message types as client-server contract
- libero: ETF wire protocol, codegen architecture, SSR patterns
- marmot: SQL-first codegen with live SQLite introspection

## Testing

```sh
gleam test                    # 103 tests: scanner, generator, parser, wire, codec, topics, session, broadcast, snapshots
gleam run -m birdie           # review snapshot changes
```
